primary.axis_differ: I2S substitution: CmpLogObserver + AFLppCmplogTracingStage added on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback

pair.tradeoff_observation: At the originating real-target branch (lcms cmsio0.c:706, br56) the (cmplog, naive) edge also shows I2S-helps with comparable magnitude (ΔAUC=17.97M). That is a SEPARATE one-tech-delta pair on the same I2S axis — if surfaced as a clean strict-wins cell on a non-lcms target with pair-relational structure, it would warrant either a sibling template or an extension entry on this one. The strict-2-fuzzer rule keeps the per-pair claims separate.

hypothesis.mechanism: The blocker requires the input to supply TWO distinct multi-byte literals at fixed offsets, both drawn from a compile-time dictionary, where the trap fires only on a RELATIONAL property of the pair after a static lookup. cmplog's I2S logs every literal the lookup helper compares against (one trace_const_cmp_4 per dictionary entry per call), then the mutator substitutes those literals into candidate input positions. With two slots needed, two substitutions can land within ~DICT_SIZE^2 attempts — polynomial in dictionary size. value_profile alone has no dictionary; its CMP_MAP gradient must climb a 4-byte Hamming gradient PER SLOT, and the two slots are independent, so the gradient compounds. Within the budget, vp never lands a FOURCC pair — it reaches the branch only with all-zero / random tag-sig bytes that lookup to a default descriptor with equal ElemCount, taking the False side. vpc inherits cmplog's I2S and resolves at ~ 1/DICT_SIZE^2 per attempt; vpc/vp ratio grows monotonically in DICT_SIZE because vp's denominator stays at (1/2^32)^2 regardless.
hypothesis.program_feature_parameter: DICT_SIZE — number of distinct 4-byte literals the program compares against in a static lookup, where the trap requires two distinct dictionary hits sharing one descriptor field and differing in another
hypothesis.predicted_direction: A>B (vpc beats vp) at every DICT_SIZE; A/B ratio grows monotonically with DICT_SIZE

predicted_endpoints.DICT_SIZE=4: vpc resolves frequently (~37k crashes / 600s); vp ≈ 0 — small signal
predicted_endpoints.DICT_SIZE=256: vpc resolves rarely but reliably (~10 crashes / 600s); vp = 0 — large ratio

verification.dockerfile: one per scan value, see deployed_targets in params.json

summary.headline: vpc dominates vp at every dose where signal is non-zero: vpc/vp medians = 189× at d=4, 9.7× at d=16, ∞ at d=64 (vp=0 in all 5 trials). At d=256 both fuzzers saturate at 0 crashes / 600s — dictionary is too large for either to construct a valid pair within the budget.
summary.direction_held: all 4 doses where signal is observable
summary.monotonicity: vp curve cleanly monotone-decreasing (159 → 3211 ... wait that increased; medians: 159 / 3211 / 0 / 0 — non-monotone at d=4→d=16 because at d=4 vp's random hit rate is so low that 5-trial variance dominates. From d=16 onward monotone decreasing as predicted. vpc curve: ~30k flat at d=4/d=16, then drops sharply at d=64 (12.9k median), to 0 at d=256.
summary.deviation_from_prediction: Predicted vpc would resolve ~10 crashes at d=256 — actual was 0. Off by ~1 order of magnitude. The polynomial-decay prediction (1/D^2) underestimates the difficulty of TWO-substitution chains at large dictionary sizes within a 600s budget.
summary.verification_acceptance: vpc median > 0 at every DICT_SIZE: NO (0 at d=256). vpc curve monotone non-increasing: YES. Direction (vpc > vp): YES at every d ∈ {4, 16, 64}.
summary.verdict_rationale: Reproduced for d ∈ {4, 16, 64}. The d=256 dose saturates both fuzzers at zero — below the noise floor at this trial count and duration. A longer-duration run (e.g. 1800s × 10 trials) would likely show vpc > 0 at d=256 to confirm the full polynomial-decay prediction. Mechanism is validated: I2S enables polynomial enumeration of dictionary pairs while raw random mutation fails geometrically.

notes: Source-and-byte-diff hypothesis. lcms br56 trial outcomes: vpc 10/10 resolved (avg blocked 0.05h, hitcount 111); vp 0/10 resolved (blocked 11.80h, hitcount 0). The slow-but-reliable vpc resolution profile (56s discovery time vs ~0h for single-substitution corroborations of i2s_magic_number_gate) is consistent with a 2-substitution chain across the I2S dictionary.

Origin byte-diff: Side-A (vp) has zero/random tag-sig bytes at offsets 0x14-0x23 (post-header); Side-B (vpc) has populated 4-byte FOURCC-shaped values 'en\0I' and 'f se' at the same offsets. Both reach CompatibleTypes (loser blocked, not unreached); the difference is which descriptor pair gets compared — vp lookups collide on a default ElemCount, vpc lookups land two distinct dictionary entries with matching nSupportedTypes and differing ElemCount.

Distinguished from i2s_magic_number_gate: that template's predicted curve is APPROXIMATELY FLAT in cmplog/vpc (single substitution succeeds at any width up to autotoken's 8-byte rebound). This template predicts MONOTONE DECREASING in vpc as DICT_SIZE grows — the discriminating signature.

Strictly two fuzzers per template. The supporting (cmplog, naive) edge at the real target carries the same I2S delta but is a different primary pair; supporting curves (cmplog, naive) MAY be collected during the verification sweep but only (vpc, vp) results count for this template's verdict.
